name: Sparse and Imbalanced Synthetic Student Response Search

search_queries.query: "DREsS" "synthetic" essays data augmentation automated essay scoring
"Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation"

inclusion_criteria: Study uses synthetic, generated, paraphrased, corrupted, or model-created student response data.
Synthetic data is motivated by limited data, imbalanced score or rubric classes, underrepresented subgroups, or sparse examples at some response level.
Study reports the role of synthetic data in training, balancing, validation, benchmarking, scorer evaluation, or synthetic-quality assessment.
Prefer primary papers, official abstracts, full-text articles, or open PDFs.
exclusion_criteria: General automated scoring studies with no synthetic response augmentation.
Studies using LLMs only as scorers with no generated response dataset.
General synthetic data methods outside educational response scoring unless they directly involve student response artifacts.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, arXiv identifier, and overlap between preprint and version-of-record metadata.

screening_decisions.title: Using GPT-4 to Augment Imbalanced Data for Automatic Scoring
screening_decisions.reason: Directly uses GPT-4 to generate minority-class science responses for automatic scoring.

screening_decisions.title: Automated Scoring of Constructed Response Items in Math Assessment Using Large Language Models
screening_decisions.reason: Directly uses Coedit-XL to augment underrepresented high-scoring constructed math responses.

screening_decisions.title: Navigating the Data Frontier in Science Assessment
screening_decisions.reason: Compares real-data collection, chatbot paraphrase, and chatbot-generated responses for chemistry reasoning scorer development.

screening_decisions.title: Improving Automated Evaluation of Student Text Responses Using GPT-3.5 for Text Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Directly addresses scarcity of student-generated text by supplementing small datasets with GPT-3.5 completions.

screening_decisions.title: Augmenting AI Scoring of Essays with GPT-Generated Responses
screening_decisions.reason: Essay-scoring augmentation study focused on sample size, score-level scarcity, and subgroup fairness.

screening_decisions.title: DREsS: Dataset for Rubric-Based Essay Scoring on EFL Writing
screening_decisions.reason: Essay dataset paper introduces CASE synthetic data to handle score imbalance and improve rubric-based AES training.

screening_decisions.title: Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Essay augmentation study evaluates human score agreement and realism before using simulated essays in scorer training.

screening_decisions.title: Automated Essay Scoring in MATLAB
screening_decisions.reason: Appears to be a demonstration article with a synthetic corpus rather than a peer-reviewed education measurement study.

screening_summary: The search found multiple direct examples where synthetic student responses supplement sparse, imbalanced, or subgroup-limited datasets. Included studies span essay scoring, EFL rubric-based essay scoring, science short constructed response, chemistry reasoning, and NAEP math constructed-response scoring.
limitations: This was a targeted exploratory pass, not a systematic review.
Citation chaining was limited to papers surfaced by search results and reference lists in the included full texts.
Some included sources are preprints or recent articles whose peer-review status differs by source.
